intro:
There is limited information on Manilkariopsis meridionalis. However, Manilkara is a genus of trees in the Sapotaceae family, and Manilkara kauki is known as a medicinal plant in some regions. 

For the sake of providing general information about plants in the Manilkara genus, Manilkara kauki has been studied for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its use in traditional medicine. 

The bioactive constituents of Manilkara kauki include flavonoids, alkaloids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. 

Manilkara kauki is traditionally used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
